symbols: white Mercedarian habit — purity, consecration, and the Order of Our Lady of Mercy
book and paper in hand — devotion to sacred study and theological learning
Mercedarian coat of arms on chest — belonging to the Order dedicated to ransoming captives
red-draped table — liturgical solemnity and the sacrificial dimension of religious life
dark background — withdrawal from the world and interiority of monastic contemplation
downcast eyes — humility and prayerful recollection
quill on the table — scholarly labor in service of the faith
description: A full-length portrait of a Mercedarian friar standing in three-quarter view against a dark background. He wears the white habit and scapular of the Order of Our Lady of Mercy, with the order's coat of arms displayed on his chest. He holds a document and paper in his hands, gazing downward in quiet contemplation. Beside him, a red cloth-draped table holds a closed book and a white quill. An inscription at the upper right identifies him as Fray Pedro Machado.
